title: [Bug]: Foundry HTTP client needs reviewed Bandit B310 suppression on urlopen
summary: The new Palantir Foundry HTTP client validates endpoint schemes and hosts before constructing requests, but Bandit still reports B310 on the urllib urlopen call during the full repository security gate.
observed: The full local check script failed in scripts/security.sh with Bandit B310 at src/nats_sinks/foundry/client.py on the request.urlopen call.
expected: The reviewed urlopen call should carry an explicit Bandit B310 suppression on the exact line Bandit evaluates, with a nearby explanation that Foundry URLs are config-validated before use.
reproduction: Run scripts/check.sh after adding the experimental Foundry sink. The security step reports B310 for the Foundry HTTP client.
failing_test: Add a focused unit test that inspects src/nats_sinks/foundry/client.py and fails unless the request.urlopen line carries a reviewed nosec B310 annotation.
impact: The defect blocks release validation for issue #150 and could encourage ad hoc security suppression unless the reviewed boundary is documented in code and protected by a regression test.
delivery_semantics: The defect is limited to static-analysis annotation placement. It does not alter Foundry request construction, sink writes, retry behavior, ACK ordering, DLQ behavior, or idempotency keys.
security: The fix must keep the existing URL validation model: only HTTPS endpoints are allowed outside explicit loopback testing, URL userinfo, fragments, and query strings are rejected, and endpoint_allowed_hosts remains available for least-privilege review.
acceptance: The Foundry HTTP urlopen line carries a reviewed Bandit B310 suppression.
A focused regression test protects the annotation placement.
Issue #150 release evidence mentions the security-gate regression and fix.
tests: Run the focused annotation regression test, scripts/security.sh, and scripts/check.sh.
documentation: No public documentation change is required beyond changelog and test-report evidence because this is a static-analysis hygiene fix inside the new Foundry connector.
closeout: Mark completed after the regression test and full local check pass with sanitized evidence.
